sentence: Be careful! The road is very _____ when it rains.
explanation: RAIN → rainy (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'having rain')

sentence: It is a _____ day. The sun is shining.
explanation: SUN → sunny (double the consonant and add -y)

sentence: The sky is very _____ today. I think it will rain.
explanation: CLOUD → cloudy (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'full of clouds')

sentence: It is very _____ outside. Take a jacket.
explanation: WIND → windy (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'having wind')

sentence: The room is very _____ . Please clean it.
explanation: DIRT → dirty (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'covered in dirt')

sentence: She is a very _____ person. She always helps others.
explanation: HELP → helpful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of help')

sentence: The garden is _____ in spring with many flowers.
explanation: BEAUTY → beautiful (change -y to -iful to make an adjective)

sentence: He is very _____ for your gift.
explanation: THANK → thankful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of thanks')

sentence: The movie was very _____ . I laughed a lot.
explanation: FUN → funny (double the consonant and add -y)

sentence: She is a _____ girl. She always tells the truth.
explanation: TRUTH → truthful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of truth')

sentence: The children are very _____ . They play all day.
explanation: PLAY → playful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of play')

sentence: My hands are _____ . I need to wash them.
explanation: STICK → sticky (add -y to make an adjective)

sentence: The soup is very _____ . It has a lot of salt.
explanation: SALT → salty (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'having salt')

sentence: Be _____ when you cross the road.
explanation: CARE → careful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of care')

sentence: The cake is very _____ . It has a lot of sugar.
explanation: SUGAR → sugary (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'having sugar')

sentence: I am _____ that you passed the exam!
explanation: JOY → joyful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of joy')

sentence: The floor is _____ . Someone spilled water.
explanation: SLIP → slippery (add -pery to make an adjective meaning 'easy to slip on')

sentence: It was a _____ night. The snow covered everything.
explanation: SNOW → snowy (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'having snow')

sentence: She gave me a _____ smile.
explanation: CHEER → cheerful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of cheer')

sentence: The baby has _____ skin.
explanation: SILK → silky (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'like silk')

sentence: He is a _____ boy. He always says 'please' and 'thank you'.
explanation: RESPECT → respectful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of respect')

sentence: The weather is _____ today. There is a lot of fog.
explanation: FOG → foggy (double the consonant and add -y)

sentence: This book is very _____ . I learned a lot.
explanation: USE → useful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of use')

sentence: The road is very _____ . There are many rocks.
explanation: ROCK → rocky (add -y to make an adjective meaning 'full of rocks')

sentence: She is a _____ student. She studies hard every day.
explanation: WONDER → wonderful (add -ful to make an adjective meaning 'full of wonder')
